rice.season: Kharif season, mainly June to November
rice.ideal_soil: Clayey or loamy soil with good water retention
how_to_grow: Prepare a puddled field before transplanting.
Use healthy seedlings around 20 to 30 days old.
Maintain proper water level during vegetative growth.
soil_care: Maintain standing water during early growth.
Ensure good leveling to avoid uneven irrigation.
pest_care: Use neem oil spray for pests.
Monitor regularly for leaf infections.

how_to_grow: Use well-drained fertile soil.
Maintain proper spacing between plants.
Irrigate during flowering and grain formation.
soil_care: Avoid waterlogging.
Maintain soil fertility with organic matter.
pest_care: Use pheromone traps.
Apply neem-based pesticides.

jute.season: Warm and humid season, mainly March to May sowing
how_to_grow: Grow in warm humid conditions.
Use alluvial or loamy soil.
Keep field moist during early growth.
soil_care: Maintain moisture during early growth.
Avoid dry conditions.
pest_care: Use organic sprays.
Remove infected plants early.

cotton.season: Kharif season, sown from April to July
cotton.ideal_soil: Deep black cotton soil (Vertisol) or well-drained loamy soil
how_to_grow: Sow seeds after last frost when soil temperature reaches 18°C or above.
Maintain row spacing of 60–90 cm and plant spacing of 30–45 cm.
Irrigate at critical stages: flowering, boll formation, and boll opening.
Apply topping (removal of terminal bud) to promote lateral branching.
soil_care: Deep ploughing before sowing to break hardpan and improve aeration.
Maintain soil pH between 6.0 and 8.0.
Avoid waterlogging as cotton roots are susceptible to rot.
pest_care: Monitor for bollworms, aphids, and whiteflies regularly.
Use yellow sticky traps to detect and control whiteflies.
Apply neem oil spray or Bt-based biopesticides for bollworm control.
Use pheromone traps for monitoring American bollworm.

coconut.season: Year-round crop; best planted at the onset of monsoon (June to September)
coconut.water_requirement: Moderate and regular water requirement; drought-sensitive
coconut.ideal_soil: Sandy loam, laterite, or alluvial soil with good drainage; pH 5.5 to 8.0
how_to_grow: Select disease-free, high-yielding seedlings (10–12 months old) from a reputed nursery.
Dig pits of 1m x 1m x 1m and fill with topsoil, sand, and compost.
Plant seedlings at 7.5m x 7.5m triangular spacing for maximum yield.
Provide regular irrigation, especially during the dry months.
Intercropping with banana, turmeric, or cocoa is beneficial.
soil_care: Mulch the basin with dry leaves or coir pith to conserve moisture.
Weed regularly around the trunk, especially in early years.
Apply lime if soil is acidic (below pH 5.5).
pest_care: Monitor for rhinoceros beetle; use pheromone traps and Metarhizium biopesticide.
Check for red palm weevil; remove and destroy infested palms.
Apply neem oil to leaf axils to deter coconut mite.
chemical_fertilizers: Borax (Boron micronutrient for preventing button shedding)
organic_suggestions: Trichoderma viride for root zone protection

papaya.season: Can be grown year-round; best planted February to March or June to October
papaya.water_requirement: Moderate water requirement; does not tolerate waterlogging
papaya.ideal_soil: Well-drained sandy loam or loamy soil; pH 6.0 to 6.5
how_to_grow: Sow seeds in polybags and transplant 45–60-day-old seedlings.
Maintain spacing of 1.8m x 1.8m for dioecious varieties.
Irrigate frequently but avoid standing water to prevent root rot.
Stake plants in early stages to protect from wind.
Thin to one healthy plant per pit after sex determination.
soil_care: Raise beds for planting in areas with heavy rainfall.
Mix farmyard manure into pits before planting.
Maintain soil moisture consistently; drip irrigation preferred.
pest_care: Use yellow sticky traps for whiteflies and aphids.
Spray neem oil solution (5 ml/L) for mite and aphid control.
Uproot and destroy plants infected with papaya ringspot virus.
chemical_fertilizers: Calcium nitrate spray for fruit quality

orange.season: Planted during June to August (monsoon); harvest from November to February
orange.ideal_soil: Well-drained sandy loam or loamy soil; pH 6.0 to 7.5
how_to_grow: Use grafted or budded plants of certified varieties like Nagpur Mandarin.
Plant at spacing of 6m x 6m in pits of 60cm x 60cm x 60cm.
Irrigate at 10–15-day intervals in summer; reduce during fruit maturity.
Prune dead and crossing branches annually after harvest.
Apply deblossoming in the first two years to establish the tree.
soil_care: Mulch the tree basin with dry leaves or straw to conserve moisture.
Apply lime if soil pH is below 6.0.
Avoid compaction; inter-till shallow to keep soil aerated.
pest_care: Monitor for citrus psylla, leaf miner, and mealybug.
Use oil emulsion spray (1%) for scale insects.
Apply Chlorpyrifos or neem oil for leaf miner control.
chemical_fertilizers: Zinc sulphate and Manganese sulphate sprays

apple.season: Planted November to January; harvest July to September depending on variety
apple.water_requirement: Moderate water requirement; needs dry weather during fruiting
apple.ideal_soil: Well-drained loamy soil with high organic matter; pH 5.5 to 6.5
how_to_grow: Grow in cool climate regions (1500–2700 m altitude); requires chilling hours (below 7°C).
Plant grafted saplings during dormancy (November to January) at 5m x 5m spacing.
Train trees using central leader or spindle bush system for maximum sunlight.
Irrigate once a week during dry periods; avoid waterlogging.
Thin fruits when marble-sized to improve size and quality of remaining fruits.
soil_care: Mulch tree basins with straw or leaf litter to prevent moisture loss.
Apply organic matter every year before bud break.
Check drainage; apple roots cannot tolerate even brief waterlogging.
pest_care: Monitor for codling moth, woolly apple aphid, and San Jose scale.
Use pheromone traps for codling moth detection.
Apply Kaolin clay sprays as a physical pest deterrent.
chemical_fertilizers: Calcium chloride spray for bitter pit prevention

muskmelon.season: Summer crop; sown February to March; also grown September to October
muskmelon.water_requirement: Moderate water requirement; reduce near harvesting for sweetness
muskmelon.ideal_soil: Sandy loam or loamy soil with good drainage; pH 6.0 to 7.0
how_to_grow: Sow 2–3 seeds per hill and thin to the healthiest plant after germination.
Maintain hill spacing of 60 cm within rows and 1.5m between rows.
Provide trellis support if growing vertically.
Reduce irrigation 10–15 days before harvest to enhance sweetness and aroma.
Pollination by bees is critical; avoid insecticide sprays during flowering.
soil_care: Prepare raised beds or ridges to improve drainage.
Add compost before planting for nutrient enrichment.
Mulch with black plastic or dry straw to retain moisture and suppress weeds.
pest_care: Watch for red pumpkin beetle, aphids, and fruit flies.
Use protein bait traps for fruit fly control.
Spray neem oil (5 ml/L) for aphid and spider mite control.
organic_suggestions: Panchagavya foliar spray for fruit development

watermelon.season: Summer crop; sown January to March; harvest May to July
watermelon.water_requirement: Moderate water requirement; reduce before harvest to improve sweetness
watermelon.ideal_soil: Sandy loam or loamy soil with good drainage; pH 6.0 to 7.0
how_to_grow: Direct sow seeds 2–3 cm deep in hills spaced 1.8m x 2m apart.
Provide full sun with at least 8 hours of sunlight daily.
Ensure bee pollination by avoiding pesticide spray during flowering.
Place straw or wood board under developing fruits to prevent rot.
Check for ripeness by tapping — a hollow thud indicates maturity.
soil_care: Raise beds to ensure drainage; watermelon is highly sensitive to root rot.
Mulch heavily with dry straw to maintain soil moisture and temperature.
Incorporate organic matter before planting.
pest_care: Use protein bait traps for fruit fly control.
Apply neem oil spray for aphids and spider mites.
Monitor for red pumpkin beetle and remove manually.
chemical_fertilizers: Calcium nitrate spray for rind strength
organic_suggestions: Jeevamrut soil drench for microbial activity

grapes.season: Pruning done December to January; harvest April to June in India
grapes.water_requirement: Moderate and regular; drip irrigation strongly preferred
grapes.ideal_soil: Well-drained loamy or sandy loam soil; pH 6.5 to 7.5
how_to_grow: Plant rooted cuttings or grafted vines during monsoon (June to August) or winter.
Train vines on bower or trellis system for airflow and sunlight.
Prune twice a year to regulate cropping and maintain vine vigor.
Apply irrigation through drip system at regular intervals.
Thin bunches for larger berry size and better market quality.
soil_care: Deep plough before planting to loosen subsoil.
Mulch interrow spaces to reduce evaporation.
Avoid waterlogging; raised bunds help manage drainage.
pest_care: Monitor for thrips, mealybug, and flea beetle.
Use blue sticky traps for thrips detection.
Apply Profenofos or Spinosad for thrips management.
Spray neem oil or insecticidal soap for mealybug control.
chemical_fertilizers: MOP (Potassium source) — high potassium demand at berry fill
Ferrous sulphate for iron deficiency correction

mango.season: Flowers February to March; harvest May to July depending on variety
mango.water_requirement: Low to moderate; dry spell before flowering is essential to trigger flowering
mango.ideal_soil: Deep, well-drained alluvial or loamy soil; pH 5.5 to 7.5
how_to_grow: Plant grafted saplings (of varieties like Alphonso, Dasheri, Kesar, Totapuri) at 10m x 10m spacing.
Dig pits of 1m x 1m x 1m before planting and fill with compost-enriched topsoil.
Irrigate young trees regularly but withhold irrigation 2 months before expected flowering.
Prune crowded and dead branches after harvest to maintain tree shape.
Intercrop with legumes in the initial years to improve soil fertility.
soil_care: Mulch tree basin with dry leaves (1m radius) to conserve moisture.
Apply compost around the drip circle annually after harvest.
Check soil pH and apply lime if soil is too acidic.
pest_care: Monitor for mango hopper, fruit fly, mealybug, and stem borer.
Use pheromone traps (Methyl eugenol) for fruit fly control.
Spray neem oil or Dimethoate for mango hopper at bud burst stage.
Band polythene sheets on trunk to prevent mealybug crawling up.
chemical_fertilizers: Zinc sulphate spray at new flush emergence
organic_suggestions: Farmyard manure (25–50 kg/tree/year based on age)
Biofertilizers: Azospirillum + PSB for young trees

banana.season: Planted year-round; harvest 9–12 months after planting depending on variety
banana.ideal_soil: Rich, well-drained loamy or clay loam soil; pH 6.0 to 7.5
how_to_grow: Use disease-free tissue-cultured plants or healthy sword suckers.
Plant at 1.8m x 1.8m spacing or use paired-row system (1.2m x 1.8m x 3.6m).
Earth up the base of plants every 4–6 weeks to provide support.
Prop the pseudo-stem with bamboo poles when the bunch emerges.
Remove ratoon suckers except one sword sucker per plant for next crop cycle.
soil_care: Mulch with dry leaves or banana trash to conserve moisture.
Ensure drainage channels in waterlogged areas.
Trench composting between rows improves long-term fertility.
pest_care: Monitor for banana weevil, thrips, and nematodes.
Drench corm with Chlorpyrifos or neem cake to control weevil.
Apply Carbofuran granules for nematode management.
chemical_fertilizers: MOP (Potassium source) — banana has very high potassium demand
Magnesium sulphate spray for correcting Mg deficiency
organic_suggestions: Pseudomonas fluorescens soil drench for Fusarium wilt suppression

pomegranate.season: Planted June to August; major crop (Mrig bahar) harvested January to February
pomegranate.water_requirement: Low to moderate; drought-tolerant once established
pomegranate.ideal_soil: Well-drained loamy or sandy loam soil; pH 6.5 to 7.5; tolerates mild salinity
how_to_grow: Plant rooted cuttings or grafted plants at 4.5m x 3m spacing.
Train as a single-stem tree or multi-stem bush form.
Regulate flowering using bahar treatment (withholding water + defoliation) for market timing.
Apply bag covers (paper or non-woven fabric) on developing fruits to prevent fruit borer and sunburn.
Harvest when fruit skin turns pink to deep red and gives a metallic sound on tapping.
soil_care: Mulch tree basin to conserve moisture.
Avoid waterlogging; ensure raised planting in heavy rainfall areas.
Add organic matter before each bahar season.
pest_care: Monitor for anar butterfly (pomegranate fruit borer) — the most serious pest.
Bag fruits at marble size to prevent borer entry.
Apply Spinosad or neem oil spray at flowering.
Use pheromone traps to monitor fruit borer population.
chemical_fertilizers: Calcium nitrate spray for rind quality
organic_suggestions: Trichoderma viride for soil-borne disease suppression

lentil.season: Rabi crop; sown October to December; harvested February to March
lentil.ideal_soil: Sandy loam to loamy soil with good drainage; pH 6.0 to 8.0
how_to_grow: Sow seeds at a depth of 3–4 cm with row spacing of 25–30 cm.
Inoculate seeds with Rhizobium leguminosarum before sowing for nitrogen fixation.
One or two irrigations at branching and pod-filling stages are sufficient.
Avoid heavy irrigation which can cause excessive vegetative growth.
Harvest when 70–80% of pods turn brown and leaves begin to drop.
soil_care: Lentil fixes nitrogen; avoid excess nitrogenous fertilizers.
Light ploughing is sufficient; deep tillage is not required.
Add phosphorus-rich compost or bone meal to improve nodulation.
pest_care: Watch for aphids and pod borers.
Spray neem oil or Dimethoate for aphid control.
Use light traps to monitor and control pod borer adults.
chemical_fertilizers: DAP or SSP (Phosphorus source — lentil requires more P than N)
Low dose of Urea only as starter nitrogen

blackgram.season: Kharif (June to September) and spring (February to March)
blackgram.water_requirement: Low to moderate; sensitive to waterlogging
blackgram.ideal_soil: Well-drained loamy or clay loam soil; pH 6.0 to 7.5
how_to_grow: Sow seeds at 3–4 cm depth with row spacing of 30–45 cm.
Treat seeds with Rhizobium and PSB biofertilizers before sowing.
Irrigate at branching, flowering, and pod development stages.
Weed once at 20–25 days after sowing.
Harvest in 2–3 pickings when pods turn black.
soil_care: Avoid waterlogged conditions; ensure field drainage.
Shallow tillage to avoid disturbing nitrogen-fixing nodules.
Incorporate green manure or previous legume residue.
pest_care: Spray neem oil for jassids, thrips, and whiteflies.
Monitor for pod borer; use Bt spray (Bacillus thuringiensis) if infestation is high.
Use yellow sticky traps for whitefly monitoring.
chemical_fertilizers: Starter dose of Urea (10–15 kg/ha only)

mungbean.season: Kharif (June to September) and Zaid/summer (March to June)
mungbean.water_requirement: Low to moderate; 4–5 irrigations during dry season
mungbean.ideal_soil: Well-drained sandy loam or loamy soil; pH 6.2 to 7.2
how_to_grow: Sow seeds at 2–3 cm depth with row spacing of 30–45 cm.
Seed treatment with Rhizobium and Thiram before sowing.
Irrigate at branching, flowering, and pod filling stages.
Weed at 15–20 days after sowing to reduce competition.
Harvest when 80% of pods are mature (yellowish-brown).
soil_care: Plough to a fine tilth for good seed-soil contact.
Avoid standing water; ridge-furrow system helps drainage.
Incorporate crop residues to improve organic matter.
pest_care: Monitor for pod borer, jassids, and bruchid beetle (in stored seed).
Apply neem oil for sucking pest control.
Use Bt formulations for pod borer management at flowering.

mothbeans.season: Kharif crop; sown June to July in arid and semi-arid regions
mothbeans.water_requirement: Very low water requirement; extremely drought-tolerant
mothbeans.ideal_soil: Sandy or sandy loam soil; well-drained; pH 6.0 to 7.5
how_to_grow: Sow seeds at 2–3 cm depth with row spacing of 30–45 cm.
Inoculate seeds with Rhizobium before sowing for nitrogen fixation.
Crop survives on rainfall; additional irrigation only if very dry.
Weed once at 3 weeks after sowing.
Harvest pods progressively as they mature, since pods ripen unevenly.
soil_care: Suitable for poor sandy soils; no heavy tillage needed.
Add minimal compost since the crop is adapted to low-fertility soils.
Avoid clay-heavy soils that retain excess water.
pest_care: Monitor for pod borer and aphids.
Spray neem oil for pest management.
Use light traps to control moths at nighttime.
chemical_fertilizers: Low or no Nitrogen fertilizer needed (nitrogen-fixing legume)

pigeonpeas.season: Kharif crop; sown June to July; harvested November to January (long-duration varieties)
pigeonpeas.ideal_soil: Well-drained sandy loam or medium black soil; pH 6.5 to 7.5
how_to_grow: Sow seeds at 3–5 cm depth with row spacing of 60–75 cm.
Treat seeds with Rhizobium and fungicide before sowing.
Provide one or two supplemental irrigations at flowering and pod fill during prolonged dry spells.
Intercropping with sorghum or maize (2:1 ratio) is a popular system in dryland farming.
Harvest when pods turn brown; thresh manually or by machine.
soil_care: Deep plough before sowing to prepare a good seedbed.
Avoid waterlogging as the crop is sensitive to root zone saturation.
Being a legume, it improves soil nitrogen for subsequent crops.
pest_care: Serious pest: Helicoverpa (pod borer) — use pheromone traps.
Apply Bt spray or NPV (Nuclear Polyhedrosis Virus) for pod borer control.
Monitor for pod fly and plume moth during pod development.
common_diseases: Sterility mosaic disease (spread by eriophyid mite)

kidneybeans.season: Kharif in hills (June to July); Rabi in plains (October to November)
kidneybeans.water_requirement: Moderate water requirement; sensitive to waterlogging
kidneybeans.ideal_soil: Well-drained fertile loamy soil; pH 6.0 to 7.0
how_to_grow: Sow seeds at 4–5 cm depth with row spacing of 45–60 cm.
Treat seeds with Rhizobium leguminosarum before sowing.
Provide trellis or stake support for climbing varieties.
Irrigate at flowering and pod development stages.
Harvest dry beans when pods are completely dry and brown.
soil_care: Incorporate compost or FYM before planting.
Avoid compact soils; good soil aeration is essential for nodulation.
Maintain consistent soil moisture but avoid waterlogging.
pest_care: Monitor for bean aphid, pod borer, and bruchid beetle.
Apply neem oil for aphid and pod borer control.
Use Bt formulation at flowering for pod borer management.

chickpea.season: Rabi crop; sown October to November; harvested February to March
chickpea.water_requirement: Low water requirement; drought-tolerant; 1–2 irrigations sufficient
chickpea.ideal_soil: Well-drained sandy loam or loamy soil; pH 6.0 to 7.0
how_to_grow: Sow seeds at 5–8 cm depth with row spacing of 30–45 cm.
Seed treatment with Rhizobium and Trichoderma before sowing.
One irrigation at pre-flowering and one at pod filling is usually adequate.
Avoid late sowing, which reduces yield significantly.
Harvest when leaves turn yellow and pods become dry and hard.
soil_care: Prepare a fine seedbed with one deep plough and two cross harrowings.
Chickpea improves soil nitrogen for subsequent cereal crops.
Avoid waterlogging; excess soil moisture triggers root diseases.
pest_care: Helicoverpa (pod borer) is the most damaging pest.
Use pheromone traps for monitoring and Bt spray for control.
Apply NPV (Helicoverpa NPV) at pod initiation for effective management.
chemical_fertilizers: Zinc sulphate if Zn deficiency present
Minimal or no nitrogen fertilizer (nitrogen-fixing legume)
organic_suggestions: Farmyard manure (3–4 tonnes/hectare incorporated before sowing)
Trichoderma seed treatment for wilt prevention

coffee.season: Planted during monsoon (June to August); main harvest November to February
coffee.water_requirement: Moderate and well-distributed; requires dry spell during flowering
coffee.ideal_soil: Deep, well-drained laterite or loamy soil rich in organic matter; pH 6.0 to 6.5
how_to_grow: Plant shade-grown coffee under silver oak or erythrina shade trees at 3m x 3m spacing.
Use certified Arabica or Robusta seedlings from a reputed nursery.
Plant in pits of 45cm x 45cm x 45cm filled with compost-enriched topsoil.
Train to a single-stem or multi-stem system through regular pruning.
Harvest ripe red cherries by selective hand-picking for best quality.
soil_care: Mulch heavily with shade tree leaf fall to build organic matter.
Inter-plant with pepper or cardamom for biodiversity and income.
Apply lime if soil pH drops below 5.5 due to acidification.
pest_care: Monitor for Coffee Berry Borer (CBB) — most critical pest; use trapping with Methanol:Ethanol lures.
Spray Beauveria bassiana biopesticide for CBB management.
Monitor for mealybug and scale insects; apply neem oil spray.
Use Yellow sticky traps for whitefly detection.
common_diseases: Coffee leaf rust (Hemileia vastatrix) — most damaging disease globally
chemical_fertilizers: Borax spray for flowering support and fruit retention
organic_suggestions: Coffee pulp compost (highly recommended — made from processing waste)
Biofertilizers: Azospirillum + PSB for young plantations
